findings.description: The CTA shape/treatment instruction (SVG button shape with gradient fill) has no corresponding rule at all in the candidate set. This is a distinct visual/component rule (CTA treatment) that was dropped entirely, not merged into either color or typography rules.

The hard_constraint color hierarchy rule lists exactly three approved hex colors (#00529B, #FAA31B, #FFC60A), but the CTA SVG gradient (u_0012) uses #FFDF55 and #FAA21B, neither of which matches the approved palette exactly (FAA21B vs FAA31B mismatch, FFDF55 entirely absent). This conflicts with the 'must' polarity hard_constraint and is not reconciled or scoped as an exception anywhere.

payload.note: Flag conflicting CTA gradient colors (#FFDF55, #FAA21B) as either a documentation error or an unstated exception scope for CTA components.

findings.description: rule_lisraya_font_roles merges the headline font rule (Agenda, general use for headlines/headers, no medium restriction) with the body copy font rule (Nunito Sans, explicitly scoped 'in print') under one undifferentiated rule. This loses the print-only scope qualifier for Nunito Sans, which is significant in a banner-rules (likely digital) document.

The hard_constraint color rule states a strict hierarchy ('must') but no governance facet (owner, exception process, applicable channels/media) is attached, despite the doc scope being 'internal_banner_rules' specifically — it's unclear if this hierarchy applies only to banners or brand-wide, and whether exceptions (like the CTA gradient) are permitted.

Font asset labels ('Agenda:' and 'Nunito Sans:' with webfont link) are present in source but not represented in any rule or asset entity; the Agenda font has no accompanying link/source reference at all, suggesting missing or incomplete asset data that isn't flagged.
